summary: Highway Rush: an endless traffic car driving game with 3D-style graphics using Three.js/React Three Fiber. Player controls a car on a highway, dodging traffic, collecting coins, and surviving as long as possible with increasing difficulty.
architectural_notes: Use Three.js / React Three Fiber for 3D rendering
Game state and high score persisted via localStorage

existing_features.title: Create a new page component at frontend/src/pages/HighwayRushGame.tsx implementing the full Highway Rush game using React Three Fiber and Three.js. The scene must render an endless scrolling highway with realistic road texture, white and yellow lane markings, and a third-person camera positioned slightly behind and above the player's car.

Implement player car controls: Arrow Left/Right and A/D keys switch lanes on desktop; on mobile, render on-screen left/right touch buttons. The highway must have at least 3 lanes. Lane switching should be smooth with a brief lateral animation.

Spawn randomized oncoming traffic cars in all lanes with varied colors and simple geometric shapes. Traffic spawn rate and speed increase progressively over time. Collision detection between the player car and any traffic car ends the game.
Implement a day/night cycle that transitions the sky, ambient lighting, and road appearance over time. At high speeds, apply a motion blur post-processing effect to the scene.

On collision, trigger: a particle explosion effect at the crash point, a screen shake animation, and a brief slow-motion effect. On near-miss (traffic car passes within a threshold distance without collision), award bonus points and briefly trigger a slow-motion effect.

Display a HUD overlay with: current score (distance-based, incrementing per frame), high score (persisted in localStorage), current speed meter, and coin count. Score and high score must be updated at game over, with the high score saved to localStorage key 'highwayRush_highScore'.

Spawn collectible coins on the highway that the player can collect by driving over them. Implement a car skins system with at least 3 skins unlockable by spending accumulated coins. The currently selected skin must persist in localStorage.
Implement difficulty escalation every 30 seconds: increase traffic density, traffic speed cap, and spawn variety. Show a brief on-screen notification when difficulty increases.

Implement all game screens: Main Menu (with Play, Car Skins, and Leaderboard/High Score sections), in-game Pause overlay (with Resume and Restart buttons), and Game Over screen (showing final score, high score, coins collected, and Restart/Main Menu buttons). Include a rewarded-ad placeholder on the Game Over screen for 'Extra Life' and an interstitial-ad placeholder zone after dismissing game over.

Implement all audio using the Web Audio API (procedural synthesis, no external files): looping engine sound that pitches up with speed, crash/explosion sound on collision, looping background music, and button click sounds. Include a mute toggle button in the HUD, with mute state persisted in localStorage.

Register the Highway Rush game on the Home page (frontend/src/pages/Home.tsx) as a new game card with an appropriate icon and description, linking to the /highway-rush route. Add the route to the TanStack Router setup in frontend/src/App.tsx.

Integrate Highway Rush with the existing player profile and XP system (frontend/src/utils/playerProfile.ts): award XP based on distance score at game over, and record win/play stats. Award an achievement for reaching milestone scores (e.g., 1000, 5000, 10000 points).

new_feature_requests.summary: Third-person camera with lane switching via keyboard (Arrow/A&D) and touch controls
Endless highway with realistic road, lane markings, and random traffic patterns
Day/night cycle, motion blur at high speed, crash particles and explosion animation, screen shake
Score system based on distance, high score saved in localStorage, speed meter UI
Coin collection system and unlockable car skins
Difficulty increases every 30 seconds, near-miss bonus points, slow motion on close calls
Main menu, pause, and restart screens
Engine sound, crash sound, background music loop, button click sounds
Ad placement zones: banner ad space, rewarded ad for extra life, interstitial after game over
Responsive design for desktop and mobile, optimized for smooth 60 FPS
